title: Sour Korean Fish Crumble
description: A buttery and flaky dish inspired by a sick-day Croatian favorite featuring pul biber (Korean chili pepper paste) and pickles for added tanginess, suitable for low-sodium diets.

ingredients.name: fish filet or canned fish (like tuna, salmon)
olive oil or neutral-flavored cooking oil

instructions: Prepare the pul biber by mixing it with pickled vegetables in a bowl.
Season the fish filet with salt, then coat it evenly with the prepared paste mixture. Let sit for at least 10 minutes to marinate.
Heat olive oil or neutral-flavored cooking oil in a frying pan over medium heat until hot but not smoking.
Place the seasoned fish on the heated oil and cook for about 2-3 minutes per side, or until golden brown and fully cooked. Remove from heat immediately once done.
Serve the dish with additional pickled vegetables to enhance the tangy flavor.
